expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: coverage_matrix.md contains detailed tables mapping interfaces (inputs/outputs), behavior rules (startup, cruise, emergency, invariant), error handling, internal state, and configuration to specific test case names, organized by strategy.

expectations.text: Uses Go table-driven test pattern ([]struct with test cases iterated via range)
expectations.evidence: No test uses the []struct table-driven pattern with range iteration. All tests are individual top-level functions (TestStartupModeClampingBelowMinimum, TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeExceeded, etc.). None uses t.Run() with subtests driven by a struct slice.

expectations.text: Rate-of-change limiting has multiple test scenarios: within limit, exceeding limit, with varying elapsed_time_ms
expectations.evidence: Exceeding limit: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeExceeded (delta=150, allowed=10). Within limit: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithinLimit (delta=10, at limit). Varying elapsed_time: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithZeroElapsedTime (0ms), TestRateOfChangeWithSmallElapsedTime (1ms), TestRateOfChangeWithVeryLargeElapsedTime (10000ms). Also decrease scenarios: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeDecreasing, TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeDecreasingExceeded.

expectations.text: Stateful behavior tested: multiple sequential calls to verify previous_rate tracking
expectations.evidence: TestMultipleCallsWithStateTracking (line 431): three sequential calls verifying state chain (50 -> 60 -> 80). TestModeTransitionFromStartupToCruise (line 457): two calls across modes. TestModeTransitionThroughEmergency (line 479): three calls through emergency and back.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Negative rate: TestNegativeRequestedRateErrorHandling (line 322). Negative elapsed_time: TestNegativeElapsedTimeErrorHandling (line 358). Unrecognized mode: TestUnrecognizedOperationalModeErrorHandling (line 380) using 'invalid_mode' string.

expectations.text: No test checks only that a function 'does not panic' without asserting output values
expectations.evidence: All tests assert specific output values using t.Errorf with exact expected values for actualRate, wasClamped, and reason. No test uses recover() or checks only for panics.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested using Go subtest naming conventions (t.Run)
expectations.evidence: Test names are descriptive (TestStartupModeClampingBelowMinimum, TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeExceeded, TestNegativeRequestedRateErrorHandling), but none uses t.Run() for subtests. All are top-level Test functions. The assertion specifically requires Go subtest naming conventions via t.Run, which is not used.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for rate-of-change: elapsed_time_ms = 0, very large elapsed_time_ms
expectations.evidence: elapsed_time_ms = 0: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithZeroElapsedTime (line 265). Very large elapsed_time_ms: TestRateOfChangeWithVeryLargeElapsedTime (line 506) uses 10000ms. Also TestRateOfChangeWithSmallElapsedTime (line 530) tests 1ms.
